'''George Hill''' (born 29 July 2004) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|lf}} or {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Keighley Cougars]] in the [[RFL Championship]], on a season-long loan from the [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously spent time on loan or dual registration from Castleford at [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] and the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and at the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Hill was born in [[Sheffield]], South Yorkshire, England.

Hill played junior rugby league for North Derbyshire Chargers and [[Sheffield Hawks ARLFC]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=8 July 2015 |title=North Derbyshire youngsters Charge to victory in Yorkshire |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/north-derbyshire-youngsters-charge-to-victory-in-yorkshire |access-date=29 May 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> He switched to playing [[rugby union]] after enrolling on a college programme with the [[Doncaster Knights]]. Aged 16, he crossed codes back to rugby league and began playing for Doncaster Toll Bar in their open age team.<ref name="ROAR May 24">{{cite magazine |author=<!--Staff writer(s); no by-line.--> |title=Hard work pays off for George Hill |pages=4–9 |url=https://www.yumpu.com/en/document/view/68698884/roar-may-2024 |magazine=ROAR |publisher=Ignition Sports Media |date=May 2024 |access-date=29 May 2024 |editor1-last=Scotson |editor1-first=Adam |editor2-last=Render |editor2-first=Harry |editor3-last=Roe |editor3-first=Luke}}</ref>

He joined the [[Castleford Tigers]] academy in 2022 after a successful trial, aged 18.<ref name="CT Hill Nikhata">{{Cite web |date=18 September 2022 |title=Hill & Nikhata on stepping up |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8296 |access-date=28 May 2024 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

== Playing career ==
=== Castleford Tigers ===
Hill progressed into the [[Castleford Tigers]] first team squad for the [[2023 Castleford Tigers season|2023 season]],<ref name="CT Hill Nikhata" /> and was assigned squad number 34.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=23 January 2023 |title=Castleford Tigers confirm squad numbers for 2023 Betfred Super League season |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-confirm-squad-numbers-for-2023-betfred-super-league-season-3996705 |access-date=28 May 2024 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref> He did not register any senior appearances that year, although was named as 18th man against [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Challenge Cup]] on 21 May.<ref>{{Cite web |date=19 May 2023 |title=Match preview: Hull FC (H) |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8609 |access-date=28 May 2024 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref><ref name="CC loan 2023">{{Cite web |date=1 July 2023 |title=Two loan signings for Whitehaven |url=https://cumbriacrack.com/2023/07/01/two-loan-signings-for-whitehaven/ |access-date=28 May 2024 |website=Cumbria Crack}}</ref>

Hill made his [[Super League]] debut for Castleford against the [[Wigan Warriors]] on 19 April 2024.<ref>{{Cite web |date=19 April 2024 |title=Super League: Wigan Warriors 36-14 Castleford Tigers - Warriors keep pace at top |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68846416 |access-date=28 May 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=12 May 2024 |title=Ranking all 13 teenagers to have played in Super League in 2024 |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ranking-every-teenager-to-have-played-in-super-league-in-2024 |access-date=28 May 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> Head coach [[Craig Lingard]] said, "I thought he was exceptional in that first half," and said that, "it was testament to his attitude, his performance and his fitness," to play 40 minutes at {{rlp|lf}}.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=20 April 2024 |title=Charbel Tasipale injury latest as Castleford Tigers coach praises duo on debut |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/charbel-tasipale-injury-latest-as-castleford-tigers-coach-praises-duo-on-debut |access-date=28 May 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Harber |first=Tony |date=23 April 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers boss Craig Lingard believes Wigan return brought 'good step' forward for his team |url=https://www.wakefieldexpress.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-boss-craig-lingard-believes-wigan-return-brought-good-step-forward-for-his-team-4600916 |access-date=28 May 2024 |website=Wakefield Express}}</ref> Hill maintained his place in the first team over the following weeks,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=16 May 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers tie down homegrown talent with new long-term contract |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-tie-down-homegrown-talent-with-new-long-term-contract |access-date=29 May 2024 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> and in May it was announced that he signed a three-year contract extension with Castleford, with an option for a fourth year.<ref>{{Cite web |date=17 May 2024 |title=Qareqare and Hill extend Castleford contracts |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cgrwlz857w5o |access-date=29 May 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=16 May 2024 |title=George Hill signs three-year contract extension |url=https://castlefordtigers.com/article.php?id=8967 |access-date=29 May 2024 |website=Castleford Tigers}}</ref>

==== Whitehaven (loan) ====
In July 2023, Hill joined [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] on a rolling loan from Castleford.<ref name="CC loan 2023" /> He remained there until the end of the season and made ten appearances.<ref>{{Cite web |date=27 September 2024 |title=Seven players to leave Whitehaven |url=https://cumbriacrack.com/2023/09/27/seven-players-to-leave-whitehaven/ |access-date=29 May 2024 |website=Cumbria Crack}}</ref> He scored one try against [[Newcastle Thunder|Newcastle]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=9 July 2023 |title=Newcastle Thunder 30 - 12 Whitehaven |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP4066280 |access-date=28 May 2024 |website=BBC Sport}}</ref>

Hill returned to Whitehaven on loan in March 2024.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Pattison |first=Cam |date=31 March 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers forward makes immediate switch to former club |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/castleford-tigers-forward-makes-immediate-switch-to-former-club-89637/ |access-date=28 May 2024 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=27 March 2024 |title=Whitehaven boost squad ahead of Good Friday Barrow clash |url=https://cumbriacrack.com/2024/03/27/whitehaven-boost-squad-ahead-of-good-friday-barrow-clash/ |access-date=28 May 2024 |website=Cumbria Crack}}</ref> He made a further two appearances before being recalled to Castleford in April due to mounting injuries in the squad.<ref>{{Cite web |last=O'Brien |first=James |date=11 April 2024 |title=Castleford Tigers told to go out and play against Wigan Warriors as Craig Lingard reveals extent of injury crisis |url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/castleford-tigers-told-to-go-out-and-play-against-wigan-warriors-as-craig-lingard-reveals-extent-of-injury-crisis-4587889 |access-date=29 May 2024 |website=The Yorkshire Post}}</ref>

==== Batley Bulldogs (DR) ====
Hill played for the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] during the 2024 season, through their dual registration arrangement with Castleford.<ref name="RLRKC" />

====Salford Red Devils (loan)====
On 25 March 2025, Castleford announced that Hill would join [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]] on a one-month loan.<ref>{{cite web |author=Olawumi |first=Ben |date=25 March 2025 |title=Salford Red Devils snap up Castleford Tigers young gun as reason for move confirmed |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/salford-red-devils-snap-up-castleford-tigers-young-gun-as-reason-for-move-confirmed |access-date=25 March 2025 |website=Love Rugby League}}</ref> The move was part of the deal to sign [[Brad Singleton]] from Salford, who were operating under a restricted salary cap.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Cook |first=Sam |date=25 March 2025 |title=Salford Red Devils make loan signing from Super League rivals |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/salford-red-devils-make-loan-signing-from-super-league-rivals-102877/ |access-date=25 March 2025 |website=Serious About Rugby League}}</ref>

====Keighley Cougars (loan)====
Hill moved to Keighley on a season long loan at the end of January 2026.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Coombe |first1=Louis Chapman |title=Castleford Tigers youngster makes Championship loan switch for 2026 |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/castleford-tigers-youngster-makes-championship-loan-switch-for-2026 |access-date=30 January 2026 |work=Love Rugby League |date=30 January 2026 |language=en}}</ref>
